title: Novavax Q4 2022 Financial Results: Record Revenue Growth and Transition to Commercial Stage
text: Novavax achieved fourth quarter 2022 revenues of $357 million, representing 61% growth compared to $222 million in Q4 2021. For the full year 2022, total revenue reached $1.9 billion, a 73% increase from $1.1 billion in 2021. The company attributed this growth to product sales of its COVID-19 vaccine, Nuvaxovid. The company reported a net loss of $182 million for Q4 2022, an improvement from the $846 million loss in Q4 2021. Full year 2022 net loss totaled $658 million versus $1.7 billion in 2021. As of December 31, 2022, Novavax held $1.3 billion in cash and equivalents, down from $1.5 billion at year-end 2021. The company raised $250 million through concurrent equity and convertible securities offerings in December 2022. New CEO John Jacobs identified three priorities: to deliver a competitive product for the upcoming 2023 fall vaccination season, to reduce rate of spend, and to leverage the technology platform. The company distributed over 100 million Nuvaxovid doses globally and secured agreements for 1.5 million additional doses from the U.S. government. Novavax also initiated Phase 2 trials for a COVID-19-Influenza combination vaccine candidate. The company disclosed substantial doubt regarding its ability to continue as a going concern through one year.
